overview: This deck leverages a steady stream of 0/1 Zombie tokens to fuel sacrifice outlets, growing Sephiroth into a massive combat threat or funneling damage through The Masamune. It aims to close games quickly with recursive pressure and sacrifice synergies, out-grinding control and combo while stabilizing against aggro through token trading and targeted removal.
win_conditions: Combat damage with a heavily buffed Sephiroth, or sacrificing a large creature/token via The Masamune for lethal.
Recursion of Sephiroth with accumulated +1/+1 counters also closes games quickly.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 1-2 lands, Sol Ring, and either Sephiroth or The Masamune.
Drop any hand with only lands, only removal, or no early mana acceleration.
Prioritize keeping 2-3 mana sources by turn 2 to cast your commander or key artifacts.

early_game: Turn 1: Play land, cast Sol Ring if available.
Turn 2: Play second land, cast Sephiroth or The Masamune.
If Sephiroth, begin token generation on upkeep.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast your commander by turn 3 at the latest, prioritizing black mana sources.
mid_game: Hoard low-cost removal and sacrifice outlets.
Avoid overcommitting to board wipes unless necessary.
Manage the graveyard by using recursion sparingly
prioritize keeping Sephiroth alive and maintaining a steady token pipeline.
Commit threats when you have enough tokens to trade favorably or when The Masamune can finish a player.
Hold back if opponents have sweepers or if you are low on resources.
Use Sephiroth's +1/+1 growth to pressure without overextending.
late_game: Combat damage with a heavily buffed Sephiroth, or sacrificing a large creature/token via The Masamune for lethal.
Recursion of Sephiroth with accumulated +1/+1 counters also closes games quickly.
Go all-in when you have 3+ tokens, a sacrifice outlet, and enough mana to cast Sephiroth's ability or Masamune's trigger.
Also commit if an opponent is at 5 or less life or if you have a clear path to combat damage.

sequencing_tips: Common mistake: Over-sacrificing tokens too early without a payoff.
Always calculate Masamune damage versus Sephiroth's combat value.
Remember that 0/1 tokens die at end of turn, so you must sacrifice them or use them before then.
don't let it die to removal unless you have a way to bring it back.
Track opponent life totals carefully, as Masamune can close games unexpectedly.

matchups.advice: Use cheap, uncounterable threats like tokens, split your spells across turns, and rely on instant-speed sacrifice outlets or protection to bypass counterspells.
Apply early pressure, use artifact hate to disrupt their mana base, and prioritize sweepers to stop their big creatures before they stabilize.
Stabilize with cheap removal, board wipes, and life gain if possible. Use Sephiroth's tokens to block and trade favorably, then pivot to a win condition once the board is clear.
Pressure with early threats, use card draw to out-value them, and exploit their lack of interaction by committing to combat and leveraging sacrifice synergies for value.

Win by out-resourcing with better token generation, superior sacrifice outlets, and timing your sweepers and removal to break their board first. Prioritize keeping Sephiroth alive longer than your opponent.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Damnation, Wrath), targeted removal (Murder, Fatal Push), and combo win conditions.
Note when opponents tap out for counters or big spells, and track how many tokens they have to anticipate their next move.
